2603.17449.title: TRiMS: Real-Time Tracking of Minimal Sufficient Length for Efficient Reasoning via RL
2603.17449.paper_summary: Large language models achieve breakthroughs in complex reasoning via long chain-of-thought sequences. However, this often leads to severe reasoning inflation, causing substantial computational redundancy. To maximize Intelligence per Token, we introduce a theoretical metric, MSL-Minimal Sufficient Length. MSL rigorously characterizes the shortest reasoning length that preserves answer correctness. We provide a recursive definition based on independently sampled sequences and prove the existence of its limit, establishing the first measurable lower bound for reasoning-chain compression. Building on an analysis of mainstream CoT compression strategies, we identify key structural factors enabling a model to approach MSL. Based on these insights, we propose TRiMS which employs the GRPO algorithm in conjunction with MSL-based estimation during training, while mitigating instabilities during the training process through dynamic batch aggregation and advantage computation using batch-level standard deviation. TRiMS achieves over 80% CoT token reduction with a minor accuracy boost across all benchmarks.
2603.17449.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型透過長鏈思考序列在複雜推理上取得突破。<lb/>然而，這常常導致嚴重的推理膨脹，造成大量的計算冗餘。<lb/>為了最大化每個標記的智慧，我們引入了一個理論度量，MSL-最小充分長度。<lb/>MSL 嚴格地描述了保持答案正確性的最短推理長度。<lb/>我們提供了一個基於獨立抽樣序列的遞歸定義，並證明其極限的存在，建立了推理鏈壓縮的第一個可測量下界。<lb/>在對主流 CoT 壓縮策略的分析基礎上，我們確定了使模型接近 MSL 的關鍵結構因素。<lb/>基於這些見解，我們提出了 TRiMS，該方法在訓練期間使用 GRPO 算法結合基於 MSL 的估計，同時通過動態批次聚合和使用批次水平標準差的優勢計算來減輕訓練過程中的不穩定性。<lb/>TRiMS 在所有基準測試中實現了超過 80% 的 CoT 標記減少，並帶來了輕微的準確性提升。
2603.17449.author: Tingcheng Bian et.al.
2603.17449.authors: Tingcheng Bian, Jinchang Luo, Mingquan Cheng, Jinyu Zhang, Xiaoling Xia, Ni Li, Yan Tao, Haiwei Wang
